channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Crews rush to recover commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice before expected snow and wind
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Just hours after finding 10 people dead in western Alaska from one of the deadliest plane crashes in the state in 25 years, authorities raced to recover their remains and the wreckage of the small commuter plane from unstable sea ice before expected high winds and snow.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: For years, the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office in suburban Indianapolis has wanted to partner with federal immigration authorities to identify and detain immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally and facing charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A 15-year-old went to a Brooklyn parade. The NYPD wrongly accused him of a mass shooting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Camden Lee was leaving high school football practice in September when he saw the photograph, splashed across the New York Police Department’s social media accounts, that would soon upend his life.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the Kennedy Center and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal prisons being used to detain people arrested in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump’s administration is using federal prisons to detain some people arrested in its immigration crackdown, the federal Bureau of Prisons said Friday, returning to a strategy that drew allegations of mistreatment during his first term.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boston lawyer found dead on houseboat as mystery woman faces murder charge
item.description: Boston lawyer Joseph Donohue suffered stab wounds on a houseboat before Massachusetts authorities arrested suspect Nora Nelson in his murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For high school sports, decisions loom: Follow Trump or state law on transgender athletes
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota's governing body for high school sports says it will follow state law — not President Donald Trump's executive order — and continue to allow transgender athletes to compete in prep athletics.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States absorb big increases in Medicaid for sicker-than-expected enrollees after COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — States are absorbing substantial increases in health care costs for the poor, as they realize that the people remaining on Medicaid rolls after the COVID-19 pandemic are sicker than anticipated — and costlier to care for.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WGN 9

item.title: Trump is trying to halt the EV charger buildout. Experts say it's not that easy
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Experts are questioning President Donald Trump’s latest effort to slow the electric vehicle charging buildout in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Mystery seed packages return to Texas
item.description: The state’s Department of Agriculture is warning residents not to open the mystery packages and report the items immediately.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston said on Friday he would take under advisement a request from 18 state attorneys general to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Naval Academy closing DEI offices to align with Trump executive orders: memo
item.description: The head of the U.S. Naval Academy says the school is closing DEI offices and ending DEI-related contracts to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slidell woman arrested in death investigation of Kansas City reporter
item.description: Louisiana law enforcement continue to investigate that death of a Kansas City reporter visiting New Orleans for Super Bowl coverage.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Southern California Edison's equipment may have started major wildfire: CEO
item.description: Southern California Edison is looking into whether its equipment is tied to the Eaton and Hurst Fires, its parent company said, though the cause has not been officially determined.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Advocates vow to fight for the rights of NYC immigrants
item.description: Some migrants in New York City are staying away from school, church, and work – to avoid arrest during an ICE immigration raid.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, ABC 4

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in California
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 20K have accepted Trump's government buyout offer
item.description: At least 20,000 federal employees have accepted what amounts to a buyout from the Trump administration, a source confirmed to The Hill on Tuesday. Employees still have until Feb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
